headline: MARA Education Loans Show 72% Non-Performing Rate
context: Majlis Amanah Rakyat disclosed that 72% of its RM14.8 billion education loan portfolio was non-performing in 2025, the worst recovery rate of any government lending agency, threatening MARA's financial sustainability.

cards.big: 72% default rate — RM10.7 billion in MARA education loans likely unrecoverable from 380,000 borrowers
cards.sub: MARA graduates default at nearly double PTPTN's 52% rate — raising questions about loan management and graduate quality

cards.big: MARA disbursed RM2.1 billion in new education loans in 2025 while collecting only RM420 million in repayments
cards.sub: At current recovery rate, MARA needs RM1.7 billion annual government injection just to maintain lending capacity

cards.big: MARA-sponsored overseas graduates show 45% underemployment rate — working below qualification level within 3 years
cards.sub: UK and Australian degree holders returning to RM2,500 starting salaries cannot service RM120,000 education debts

cards.big: MARA's loan recovery unit has 28 staff managing 380,000 accounts — 1 officer per 13,571 borrowers
cards.sub: No automated salary deduction mechanism unlike PTPTN — relies entirely on voluntary repayment and manual follow-up

cards.big: Sending students overseas on loans they'll never repay isn't uplifting Bumiputera education — it's creating a debt trap disguised as opportunity
Should MARA convert to a scholarship model funded by waqf endowment rather than loans with 72% default rates?
